key_insight: The paper defines a canonical labelling for finite solvable groups, making two such groups isomorphic exactly when their canonical presentations match.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable group identification and isomorphism testing in computational algebra systems, supporting symbolic computation pipelines such as GAP.
method: Build a canonical presentation can(G) for each finite solvable group.
Use group cohomology ideas to construct the presentation and an explicit isomorphism G → can(G).
Incorporate automorphism-group algorithms to make the construction canonical and computationally tractable.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a proof-of-concept implementation in GAP and discusses bottlenecks and open research questions rather than large-scale benchmark results.
watch_item: The approach may face significant computational bottlenecks in automorphism and cohomology computations, especially for larger or more complex groups.
paper_title: Computing canonical labellings of finite solvable groups
